steps.title: Translate percentage growth to a multiplier
steps.content: When a quantity grows by 4% each period, we multiply by 1.04 each time, not 0.04. After one month, we have the original 200 plus 4% of it: this equals $200 \times (1 + 0.04) = 200 \times 1.04 = 208$ customers.

steps.title: Apply exponential growth formula
steps.content: When we multiply by the same factor each month, the total grows exponentially. After 1 month: $200 \times 1.04$. After 2 months: $200 \times 1.04^2$. After $m$ months: $c = 200(1.04)^m$. That matches choice B — B is correct.
